lighting.type: Architectural ambient composite — concealed LED cove perimeter uplighting, recessed downlight arrays, tungsten-balanced accent spotlighting; secondary register of full-saturation theatrical color wash in immersive exhibition spaces
lighting.direction: Predominantly overhead omnidirectional with perimeter cove fill producing near-shadowless mid-field; lateral volumetric wash in chromatic rooms; single-spot nadir key in close-up macro register
lighting.quality: Soft, diffused with gradual highlight rolloff across polished stone and gilded surfaces in warm retail spaces; high-contrast with sharply crushed shadows and blown-out color fields in theatrical immersive rooms; clinical flat-fill in white/neutral retail environments

grain.film_stock: none — high-resolution digital capture, ISO 100–400 equivalent, zero visible luminance noise across all 49 frames

lens.focal_length: 16–24mm rectilinear wide-angle (dominant architectural register); 90–105mm macro-telephoto (close-up detail register)
lens.aperture: f/5.6–f/11 architectural interiors; f/2.8–f/4 close-up detail

atmosphere: luminous incandescent bloom emanating from concealed cove sources, halating across warm stone and lacquered surfaces
lifted blacks with fully preserved shadow detail in warm retail spaces — no crushed shadow tones
gentle specular highlight rolloff on travertine, onyx, and brushed gold metalwork
micro-reflections and lens flare suppression on glass showcases and polished resin floors
monochromatic saturated color cast flooding entire frame volume in theatrical immersive rooms — crimson, cobalt, and cyan channels independently dominant
mirror-floor infinity plane reflection collapsing spatial depth in dark exhibition volumes
atmospheric layering through glazed partitions, archways, and enfilades creating receding tonal planes
clinical near-zero shadow flatness with cool blue-white color cast in white retail environments
shallow bokeh with creamy out-of-focus highlight discs in macro close-up register
dusk blue-hour ambient color contamination and motion-blur streaking in exterior architectural shots
style_summary: The corpus operates across three distinct photographic registers unified by a shared commitment to high-resolution, grain-free digital capture and precision tonal management. The dominant register — approximately 60% of the collection — is warm-palette luxury architectural photography shot on rectilinear 16–24mm wide-angles at f/8–f/11 with zero depth-of-field falloff: concealed LED cove strips and recessed downlights produce a soft omnidirectional fill across champagne ivory, travertine, and gilded amber surfaces, with lifted blacks, preserved shadow texture, and a gentle highlight rolloff that flatters specular materials. A second theatrical register — roughly 30% — breaks aggressively into saturated monochromatic color-wash territory: crushed shadow fields in near-black, full-saturation crimson or cobalt volumetric illumination, and mirror-floor reflections that dissolve architectural geometry into infinite chromatic abstraction. A minor third register applies 90–105mm macro-telephoto compression with wide aperture and shallow depth of field to isolate material texture in extreme close-up, isolating subjects against tonally graduated, near-dark backgrounds. Post-processing across all registers favors clean tonal transitions, restrained midtone saturation in warm sequences, and hyper-controlled highlight recovery — a signature of commercial architectural and editorial photography for luxury maisons.
